import sys

def query(a):
    print('? ' + ' '.join(map(str, a)))
    sys.stdout.flush()
    return int(input())

n = int(input())
p = [0] * (n + 1)

for i in range(1, n + 1):
    a = list(range(1, n + 1))  # a[j] = j (1-indexed: a[j-1]=j)
    a[i-1] = i + 1 if i < n else 1
    # Use all same value except position i
    a = [n] * n
    a[i-1] = 1
    k = query(a)
    if k == i:
        # s[i] = p[i]+1, s[k2] = p[k2]+n, collision: p[i]+1 = p[k2]+n -> impossible since p[i]<=n, p[k2]>=1
        pass

# Better: binary search per position
p = [0]*(n+1)
remaining = set(range(1, n+1))

for i in range(1, n+1):
    a = [n]*n
    a[i-1] = 1
    k = query(a)
    if k == 0:
        # no collision, all s distinct. s[j]=p[j]+n for j!=i, s[i]=p[i]+1
        # p[i]+1 != p[j]+n for all j!=i => p[i] != p[j]+n-1, always true since p[j]+n-1 >= n >= p[i] only if p[j]=1,p[i]=n
        # Actually if p[i]+1 = p[j]+n => p[i]-p[j]=n-1 => p[i]=n,p[j]=1
        # So no collision means p[i]!=n or there's no j with p[j]=1 (impossible)
        # Hmm this doesn't directly give p[i]
        pass

print("! " + " ".join(map(str, p[1:])))
sys.stdout.flush()

for i in range(1, n + 1):
    a = list(range(1, n + 1))  # a[j] = j (1-indexed: a[j-1]=j)
    a[i-1] = i + 1 if i < n else 1
    # Use all same value except position i
    a = [n] * n
    a[i-1] = 1
    k = query(a)
    if k == i:
        # s[i] = p[i]+1, s[k2] = p[k2]+n, collision: p[i]+1 = p[k2]+n -> impossible since p[i]<=n, p[k2]>=1
        pass

# Better: binary search per position
p = [0]*(n+1)
remaining = set(range(1, n+1))

for i in range(1, n+1):
    a = [n]*n
    a[i-1] = 1
    k = query(a)
    if k == 0:
        # no collision, all s distinct. s[j]=p[j]+n for j!=i, s[i]=p[i]+1

    p = [0] * (n + 1)

            remaining_val.remove(val)

import sys

def query(a):
    print('? ' + ' '.join(map(str, a)))
    sys.stdout.flush()
    return int(input())

def solve():
    n = int(input())
    p = [0] * (n + 1)
    known = set()
    
    for i in range(1, n + 1):
        if p[i] != 0:
            continue
        # Try to find p[i]: send query with a[j]=n for all j, a[i]=c
        # s[j]=p[j]+n for j!=i, s[i]=p[i]+c
        # collision at i means p[i]+c = p[j]+n for some j!=i
        # Try each c from 1 to n
        for c in range(1, n + 1):
            a = [n] * (n + 1)
            a[i] = c
            k = query(a[1:])
            if k == i:
                # p[i] + c = p[j] + n for some j
                # p[i] = p[j] + n - c
                # s[i] = p[i] + c, and this equals p[j]+n
                # We need to find which value p[i]+c is
                # p[i]+c must equal n+v for some v in remaining values
                # Since p[j]+n for j!=i covers all p values except p[i] shifted by n
                # p[i]+c = n + p[j] means p[i] - p[j] = n - c
                # We don't know p[j] yet...
                # But we know p[i]+c is a duplicate in s
                # s values for j!=i are p[j]+n, ranging over all p values +n except p[i]+n
                # So p[i]+c = p[j]+n means p[i]-p[j]=n-c
                # If c < n: p[i] > p[j] (since n-c > 0 means p[i]=p[j]+(n-c))
                # Hmm, still need p[j]
                break
    
    print('! ' + ' '.join(map(str, p[1:])))
    sys.stdout.flush()

solve()
